privileged_info_summary: The synthetic CT volume used in this task features a liver with a mean attenuation of 58.0 HU and a spleen with a mean attenuation of 52.0 HU. The classification criteria for a 'Normal' (non-steatotic) liver are a liver-to-spleen (L/S) ratio of 1.0 or greater and a liver attenuation of 40 HU or greater.
